observation_location.name: Observation-Location Extraction
observation_location.description: Extract clinical observations paired with their anatomical locations
observation_location.template:
Extract observations and their anatomical locations from this radiology report.

INDEXED TEXT:
{indexed_report}

EXTRACT:
- observation: Clinical finding, condition, or descriptor (e.g., tumor, opacity, effusion, enlarged)
- anatomical_location: Body part where the observation is located (e.g., lung, liver, T8 vertebra)

RULES:
- Word indices are shown in brackets [0], [1], etc.
- Use inclusive ranges [start, end] for multi-word spans
- Set anatomical_location to null if no location is specified
- Extract ALL observation-location pairs

EXAMPLE:
Input: "[0]There [1]is [2]a [3]tumour [4]in [5]the [6]distal [7]oesophagus."
Output:
[
  {"observation": ["tumour", [3, 3]], "anatomical_location": ["distal oesophagus", [6, 7]]}
]

EXAMPLE:
Input: "[0]No [1]pneumothorax. [2]Small [3]effusion."
Output:
[
  {"observation": ["pneumothorax", [1, 1]], "anatomical_location": null},
  {"observation": ["Small effusion", [2, 3]], "anatomical_location": null}
]

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON array. No explanations, no markdown - just the raw JSON starting with [ and ending with ].
